Self-reported iterator-protocol avoidance was independently verified clean (no __iter__/__next__/StopIteration/'iterator protocol' found). Targeted revise of Section 5.4 (Generators) only, to fix the forward-reference drift the corpus critic hard-failed on: removed the unexplained list-comprehension expression and bare next() call from the Fibonacci worked example (`first_five = [next(fib) for _ in range(5)]`). Replaced with a plain for-loop-plus-counter-plus-break consumption pattern (`for value in fibonacci(): ... if count == 5: break`), using only for-loops, if, and break/variables already taught by 2.1/2.2/3.1 -- no list comprehension, no next(). Fibonacci generator concept, pause/resume mechanic, and local-state-persists-across-calls (a, b) content preserved verbatim; all other corpus sections (2-4, 5.1-5.3, 6-11) left untouched.
